paras.text: Library ke corridor me bheed thi. {{A}} apni assignment ki file sambhaalte hue jaldi me tha, tabhi kisi se takra gaya — files bikhar gayi.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (gusse me) Dekh ke nahi chal sakte kya?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Sorry sorry! Actually main... (files uthate hue) ...I should have watched my way, main soch raha tha assignment ke baare me.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (files uthate hue, thodi shant hoke) Arre wah — tumne 'should have' use kiya! Bahut log ye galat bolte hain. Waise sun, kal jo geography trip thi, tum aaye kyu nahi?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Yaar main could have come, but ghar pe kaam tha.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha ye batao, us din wali lecture samajh aayi jo tum miss kar gaye the?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Nahi yaar, bilkul samajh nahi aati agar tumhari notes na milti. I couldn't have understood it without your notes Thank god tumne share kiye.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Waise kal maine tumhe teen missed call diye the, baarish ho rahi thi, umbrella nahi thi.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sharminda) Sorry yaar, phone silent pe tha! Agar maine call sun li hoti, I would have come to pick you up turant.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Koi baat nahi, socha shayad busy ho. You might have been in some class, waise bhi tum hamesha headphones laga ke ghoomte ho.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (hasm ke) Sahi pakda! Waise ek cheez bata — mera tiffin aaj gayab tha, dhoondh dhoondh ke thak gaya.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue, table ke neeche jhaank ke) Arre wah, koi to bahut bhookha tha — someone must have taken it jab tum idhar udhar the!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Waise sach batau, kal raat 2 baje tak jaaga, kyunki I had to finish that assignment, warna marks kat jaate.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hairan) Kya?! Wo to open-book quiz tha, teacher ne class me bola tha! You needn't have prepared so much!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (sar pakadte hue) Mujhe pata hi nahi tha! Ek min... itni der se main tumse baat kar raha hoon aur socho zara — is poore conversation me kitne alag-alag 'past modals' aa gaye!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Wah, dekha! 'should have' regret ke liye, 'could have' missed ability, 'couldn't have' impossible past, 'would have' imagined result, 'might have' uncertain guess, 'must have' strong deduction, 'had to' real obligation, 'needn't have' bekaar mehnat — sab ek hi din me use kar liye tumne, bas pata nahi tha!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) To matlab ye sab roz ki zindagi me already use ho rahe the, bas naam nahi pata tha kisi ka?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul! Bas ek trick yaad rakho — jab 'pachtawa/regret' ho: should have. Jab 'possible tha par nahi kiya': could have. Jab 'possible hi nahi tha': couldn't have. Jab 'imagined result': would have. Jab 'guess/uncertain': might/may have. Jab 'evidence-based pakka guess': must have. Jab 'real majboori': had to. Jab 'bekaar mehnat ki': needn't have.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook nikalte hue) Ruko ruko, ye sab likh leta hoon, warna kal fir bhool jaunga aur phir tumhe 'should have written it down' bolna padega!

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (hasm ke, uthte hue) Dekha, khud hi seekh gaye! Chalo ab canteen chalte hain, aaj ka tiffin mera treat — but agli baar bag band karna, warna phir 'someone must have taken it' hi sunna padega!

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'You should watched your way while walking.'
quiz.answer: You should have watched your way while walking.
quiz.explain: Past ke regret ke liye 'should' ke saath 'have + V3' zaroori hai, sirf 'should + V1' nahi.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'I can attend the meeting yesterday but I was busy.'
quiz.answer: I could have attended the meeting yesterday but I was busy.
quiz.explain: Past ki ability jo use nahi hui, uske liye 'could have + V3' chahiye, sirf 'can' nahi.

quiz.q: Fill: Without your help, I ___ (not/finish) this project on time.
quiz.explain: 'Couldn't have + V3' dikhata hai ki kaam bina help ke possible hi nahi tha.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'If she had studied harder, she will pass the exam.'
quiz.answer: If she had studied harder, she would have passed the exam.
quiz.explain: Imagined past condition ka result batane ke liye main clause me 'would have + V3' aata hai.

quiz.q: Fill (uncertain guess about the past): He isn't answering calls. He ___ (be) asleep.
quiz.answer: might have been / may have been
quiz.explain: Jab past ke baare me pakka pata nahi, sirf guess/possibility hai, tab 'might have' ya 'may have + V3' use hota hai.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'The lights are off, so they must went out.'
quiz.answer: The lights are off, so they must have gone out.
quiz.explain: Evidence-based deduction ke liye 'must have + V3' chahiye, sirf 'must + V1' nahi.

quiz.q: Fill (past obligation, no choice): Yesterday there was no bus, so we ___ (walk) to college.
quiz.explain: Past me zaroori majboori/obligation batane ke liye 'had to + V1' use hota hai — koi option nahi tha.

quiz.q: Fill (did something unnecessary): He ___ (not/buy) a new bag; his old one was fine too.
quiz.explain: Kaam kiya gaya, par baad me pata chala zarurat nahi thi — isliye 'needn't have + V3'.

quiz.q: Mixed — 'I ___ (regret, past) told him the truth, but I was scared.' Which modal fits — should have / could have / needn't have?
quiz.answer: should have — I should have told him the truth, but I was scared.
quiz.explain: Ye ek pachtawa (regret) hai kisi zaroori kaam ko na karne ka, isliye 'should have + V3' sabse sahi fit hai.

quiz.q: Mixed — Match the meaning: 'must have' vs 'had to'. Which one is a GUESS and which one is a FACT/obligation?
quiz.answer: 'Must have' = guess/deduction based on evidence. 'Had to' = real fact/obligation, no guessing involved.
quiz.explain: Dono past ki baat karte hain, but 'must have' pakka certain nahi hota (sirf strong anumaan), jabki 'had to' ek real, confirmed majboori hoti hai.
